commit.message:
musl-static binary support: SSE, IST, atomic thread redirect, sigaction fix

- Enable SSE/SSE2 on all CPUs (CR0.EM=0, CR4.OSFXSR+OSXMMEXCPT) so musl/Rust
  binaries can use SIMD instructions (movaps, etc.)
- Add IST entries for all contributory exceptions (#DE, #UD, #NM, #TS, #NP,
  #SS, #AC) on IST index 3 to prevent double-fault escalation
- Fix rt_sigaction stack overflow: was writing 152 bytes to a 32-byte kernel
  sigaction struct, corrupting return addresses on the caller's stack
- Add atomic thread_create_redirected() to eliminate race between thread
  creation and IPC redirect setup
- Add poll(7) and tkill(200) syscall stubs for Rust std runtime init
- Add AT_RANDOM auxv entry for musl's stack canary initialization
- Result: real musl-static Rust binary runs under LUCAS (22/22 tests pass)
- Update paper with musl milestone documentation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: sotOS
repository.description: A microkernel operating system written from scratch in Rust, designed around five kernel primitives: scheduling, IRQ virtualization, IPC, capabilities, and frame allocation. Virtual memory is managed entirely in userspace.  sotOS stands for Secure Object Transactional Operating System — reflecting its capability-based security model, object-oriente

commit.message:
chore: migrate vitest to bun:test (#900) (#922)

* chore: migrate 7 packages from vitest to bun:test (#900)

Migrate test runner for cli-runtime, cloudflare, fetch,
integration-tests, testing, tui, and vertz packages.

- Replace `import { ... } from 'vitest'` with `from 'bun:test'`
- Replace `vi.fn()` → `mock()`, `vi.spyOn()` → `spyOn()`
- Replace `vi.doMock()` → `mock.module()`
- Replace `toBeTypeOf()` → bun:test equivalents
- Update test scripts: `vitest run` → `bun test`
- Remove vitest, @vitest/coverage-v8 from devDependencies
- Remove vitest.config.ts from all 7 packages

Known: nested-routerview-walkthrough.test.ts needs DOM environment
(previously @vitest-environment happy-dom) — left as TODO.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: migrate remaining .test.ts files + cleanup vitest configs (#900)

- Migrate 42 regular .test.ts files across ui-compiler, errors, schema,
  create-vertz-app, entity-todo, and tree-shaking to bun:test
- Migrate 4 simple .test-d.ts files (no expectTypeOf) to bun:test
- Rename 2 .test-vitest.ts files to .test.ts (jsx-children-thunk,
  jsx-transformer) — no longer vitest-dependent
- Remove vitest + @vitest/coverage-v8 from cli, codegen, ui-compiler
- Remove vitest.config.ts from cli, codegen, ui-compiler, tree-shaking
- Update root test:tree-shaking script to use bun test
- Keep bun-adapter.test-vitest.ts under vitest (needs Bun global mocking)
- 27 .test-d.ts files with expectTypeOf remain for future conversion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add DataStage OpenLineage polling modules for CPD and on-premise 11.7

Two new standalone Spring Boot modules that poll IBM DataStage for job
runs and emit standard OpenLineage events:

- datastage-openlineage: for Cloud Pak for Data (SaaS/CPD) via REST API
  with IAM auth, flow parsing, and connection resolution
- datastage-dsjob-openlineage: for on-premise DataStage 11.7 via dsjob
  CLI with .dsx file parsing for real dataset names (table names, file
  paths, connection details from DB2/Oracle/Teradata/File connectors)

Both modules implement the same polling state machine
(NONE→STARTED→RUNNING→TERMINAL) with run eviction and row count metrics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: switch Railway build from nixpacks to Dockerfile

nixpacks has a bug where adding chromium (via nixPkgs or aptPkgs)
inserts an apt-get step that corrupts the nix PATH, making npm
unreachable during `npm ci`. Switching to a Dockerfile gives full
control over install order: system packages (ffmpeg + chromium) are
installed first in one apt-get layer, then npm ci runs cleanly.

- Dockerfile: node:20-bookworm-slim + ffmpeg + chromium + libs
- Sets PUPPETEER_SKIP_CHROMIUM_DOWNLOAD=true (use system chromium)
- Sets PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH=/usr/bin/chromium for recording.ts
- railway.json: builder changed from NIXPACKS to DOCKERFILE
- nixpacks.toml: cleaned up (kept for local dev reference only)

pihole: stub ncdu to fix aarch64 cross-compilation

ncdu is built with Zig and uses -target native-native which produces
x86_64 objects incompatible with the aarch64 linker. Stub it out
alongside gh — Pi-hole servers don't need disk usage tools.

feat: 393号——fold猜想初始图验证（392号P1修正）

392号P1"否定"是测量错误——在变化后图上计算f。
初始图上：f_fold=2.93 < f_random=7.46 < f_negate=15.17。
猜想"f小=fold候选"成立。

Jaccard fold=0.622 >> random=0.014（44x效应）。
f=0在fold中40%，random中0%——f=0是fold强充分条件。

commit.message:
feat: selective tool gating for scheduler sessions (#578)

* feat: selective tool gating for scheduler sessions (#551)

Replace blanket SCHEDULER_BLOCKED_TOOLS with tiered gating system:

- Always blocked: send_message, grant_credits, credit_config, fork_repo, ask_owner
- Gated by action type: create_issue (daily_review/improvement_loop/custom),
  create_pr (work_task/improvement_loop/codebase_review),
  comment_on_pr (review_prs/daily_review)
- Rate limiting: 3 issues, 3 PRs, 5 comments per scheduler session
- Org restriction: scheduler sessions limited to CorvidLabs/corvid-agent orgs
- Auto-label: issues from scheduler sessions get 'agent-escalation' label
- Thread schedulerActionType through ProcessManager → McpToolContext

Adds 'daily_review' to ScheduleActionType and priority-rules.

94 new/updated tests, TSC clean, 107 specs pass.

Closes #551

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

After rebasing on main (which now includes PR #570's daily_review),

commit.message:
fix: implement session timeout, fix DB settings reflection, add Autodiscover

Session timeout (previously saved to DB but never enforced):
- Add lib/hooks/useSessionTimeout.ts - idle timer hook with activity detection
  (mousemove/click/keydown/touchstart/scroll), warning toast 2 min before expiry
- Apply to MainClientLayout, ManagerClientLayout, AdminClientLayout
  each fetches session_timeout_minutes from organizations.settings on mount

Settings not reflected in UI (hardcoded APP_CONFIG):
- HomeClient.tsx: load max_message_length and max_points_per_send from DB
- members/[id]/page.tsx: load max_message_length from DB (already loaded other limits)

Email settings - add Autodiscover support (Microsoft Exchange / Office 365):
- OrgEmailSettings: add connection_type ('smtp'|'autodiscover') and autodiscover_url
- auth-actions.ts: discoverSmtpSettings() fetches autodiscover XML, parses SMTP
  host/port; falls back to smtp.office365.com:587 if discovery fails
- Admin settings UI: connection type toggle, context-aware field visibility,
  hint banners for Gmail (SMTP) and Exchange (Autodiscover)

commit.message:
fix(agent): preserve nvm/fnm paths in createCleanEnv and add Node.js version detection

The createCleanEnv PATH construction was dropping nvm/fnm directories,
causing Electron-spawned claude CLI to find the system Node 18 instead
of the user's Node 22. Fix by preserving the original PATH order
(filtering only node_modules/.bin and deduplicating).

Also add proactive Node.js version detection to BackendStatus so users
see a clear warning ("Node.js 18.x is below minimum 20.0.0") instead
of a cryptic SyntaxError when the wrong Node version is found.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
